title: ProtocolLab Public Contract Repository Architecture
purpose: Describe the public repository boundary after removing runner and runtime implementation assets.
design_summary: The public repository is organized around authored contracts and declarative examples. Implementation repositories consume those contracts and provide executable behavior outside the public tree.
data_and_state_considerations: The public repository contains static contract documents only. Runtime state, retained artifacts, package stores, controller state, worker state, and deployment state belong to implementation repositories or hosted services.
edge_cases_and_constraints: Scenario-pack fixtures may be metadata-only and must not require script payloads.
Runtime package contracts may describe package payload expectations without committing executable payloads to this repository.
Traceability support files must point back to SpecTrace artifact fields rather than becoming an alternate trace model.
alternatives_considered: Keeping a public reference runner was rejected because it implies a canonical implementation surface.
Keeping validation scripts was rejected because it leaves runnable automation in the public repository.
Duplicating moved code into another public folder was rejected because it preserves the implementation repository shape.
risks: Existing documentation may still mention historical runner behavior unless kept aligned with this architecture.
Downstream implementation repositories need to consume schemas and fixtures directly because public executable validators were removed.
